focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 13

The pub was a Muggle place near Charing Cross Road, a narrow room with uncomfortable chairs and middling beer and no associations. She and Ron had started using it after the divorce because neither of them had ever kissed or argued or decided anything in this building, which was exactly why it worked. There was no memory to navigate around. It was just a room.

She'd Apparated into the alley behind the used bookshop three doors down and walked the rest, her coat pulled close against the December cold. The Floo would have been easier, but the Floo was unreliable now. There had been three failures in the last week alone, connections dropping mid-transit, and she didn't fancy rematerializing in someone's kitchen in Birmingham because the network had lost her routing. So she had Apparated, with the familiar squeeze and the ringing in her ears afterward, and then London came back at her all at once: damp pavement under her boots, diesel from a bus taking the corner too fast, wet cardboard in the bookshop bin, a cyclist swearing at a van. After weeks of controlled quiet, the city felt almost aggressively public. It wasn't bad. It was only more than she was used to.

Ron was already there. These days he was always early, a habit he'd acquired only after the separation. At the sight of her, he stood, which was new too, some politeness he'd adopted lately that she hadn't asked for and didn't correct because correcting it would mean discussing it, and discussing it would mean admitting the shape of their interactions had changed.

"Hey." He kissed her cheek. His face was cold. "You look — you look good."

It came out slightly wrong, the compliment carrying too much weight because he'd had to decide to say it, and the decision was audible. It wasn't worth rescuing....
open_hooks_to_carry: Draco's confusion and hurt at her withdrawal — he interprets it as rejection and retreats into his own distance
The Manor responds to their mutual withdrawal: corridors lengthen, archive won't open, the house goes cold
The scar reveal: glamour flickers or sleeve rides up during examination. MUDBLOOD carved in her forearm. He touches it. She flinches. His hand stays
The scene that follows: the most intimate so far. No kiss. He traces the letters. The silence. His honesty and her question

payoffs_to_land.description: The scar she has been hiding since Ch1 is seen by the person whose house it was carved in. The concealment habit established across multiple chapters makes the exposure land with full weight

prev_tail_excerpt:
...rk ledger sat squared beside the private notebook she should never have started and definitely should not have used tonight. The alcove in the south wall was still visible, though dimmer now that the archive was holding on residual grace rather than active paired presence. It could have been any ordinary working room if she had not known better.

She knew better, and tomorrow she would sit across from him again. She would ask for his arm. She would put her hand on the Mark with the folio's lines still snagging in her head and the damaged clause unresolved. She would take the tea he brought and let the house go on answering them while he still didn't know what the machinery was called.

Verification, she thought, and almost said it aloud just to hear how thin it sounded. If verification had really been the point, she would already have pulled him back to the south wall or been writing to the Ministry for a second reader. Instead Hermione pulled the private notebook toward her, tore out the page with the copied clauses, folded it twice, and slid it into the inner pocket of her cardigan. Then she put the notebook back under the maps, closed the work ledger over the safe figures, and extinguished the brighter of the two desk lamps so the room would not look, at a glance, as though anything had happened there at all.

Nothing had stopped her from telling him. The missing words in the damaged line made the silence easier. So did fourteen-F. So did the fact that tomorrow's data really did matter. But by the time any of that came to hand, she had already heard herself ask the smaller question instead.

By morning she would turn that smaller choice into procedure: the farther table, the brisker tone, distance she could call caution if he noticed and fear if she was being honest.
state_deltas_required: Hermione's withdrawal is incomprehensible to Draco; the scar reveal during a Mark examination strips the last layer of concealment between them
He has seen the full cost of what happened in his house. The power dynamic shifts — she is no longer the competent researcher helping the damaged heir. They are both marked
